Diary of Jeremy Belpo, Cateca Academy 8-grade student, October 9th.

A few weeks ago, I was hunting for parts to finish building my miniature robots. I couldn't find anything around here I could use, so I decided to rummish for scrap in the abandoned factory, not far from the academy. I figured that I could find plenty of cool old mechanical stuff left inside there that would be useful.

and I wasn't disappointed. It was unbelievable. I stumbled into some sort of complex with an entire computer lab with scanners, and especially this totally intense mainframe. For the moment, I haven't told anyone. It's my little secret. It's the coolest thing that's ever happened to me. And that wasn't all. That night, even though I was scared stiff, I decided to start up the computer. I hope I'm not gonna regret this in a minute. Okay? Go.
Now, let's see what you've got under the hood, my friend. Wow! What's all this? A video game? Who are you? Where are you?

Uh, are you? Oh, I'm extern. That depends, wants to know. Adela Robya, his brand new roommate. What? I'm new at the Academy and the principal told me to move in with you. Uh, latch on to me, sounds more like it. Relax, I'm totally cool. Trust me, you'll see you won't even know I'm there. I sure hope so. Look, we don't exactly go way back. So just for now, let's take things nice and slow, okay? Okay, nice and slow.

I ain't allergic sweetheart. I've got something very important to tell you. Important like, uh, I lost my mascara or my pink t-shirt round. No, nothing is serious about that. It's something about you and me, or it. If you want to know more, come by my dorm tonight at 8. I'm counting on you. Oh, Rick, sweet heart. Close the gunbook and babe.

Sissy, she's the principal's daughter. Guy's flipper her, but she's only flipped over me. That's pretty cool. Cool. She's totally brain dead in a leech as well. In that case, it's pretty lame. And the winner says, I don't have frogs like, that's just the way I walk. You get it? No. But what I am beginning to get is that you're determined not to give me a break. We're gonna have to get to know each other. And you'll see by the end of the day, you won't be able to do it without me.

Artificial intelligence? Can you hear me? Yes, but would you mind calling me something else, please? Okay. How do you like the name Maya? Maya? I like that. But what I'd really like is for you to tell me what I'm doing in this virtual universe. That's not so easy. But I've already got a couple of answers. You're part of a virtual environment called the Oko. And you've got to try to get out of there. Look around for a door or something like that.
Jeremy, I can't find anything. Jeremy, I... This is where you have to see this. I love it. You think you can give me a visual? I'll try.
Huh? Maya, there isn't just a forest out there. There's an entire world. I count four sectors. Each one is different. Uh, Jeremy? I'm not all alone out here. There are some animals.
That's fantastic. This virtual world has an entire ecosystem with virtual living creatures going to get over you. Jimmy! They're attacking me! Run away, Maya! Get back to the tower! Maya! Hurry up!

Your life points are regenerating. You know what I was thinking? You know you're girlfriend there? Sisie? She's not my girlfriend. Cool! So that means she's available. I'm telling you, she's lame. Plus no offense, but you're not her type. Oh, yeah, I hear that a lot. They don't plot. Oh, huh? Hey, Velva, you okay?

I'll take him to the infirmary. Tell the teacher, okay? Nothing too serious. Just a minor electrical shock and some superficial burns. He wanted some juice. Well, you got some very funny stirring, but thank you all the same. Don't mention it. I'll come by to check on you tonight after my martial arts class.

How are you all? I'm happy to see so many of you here tonight. Calaripayat, Kogosuku, Jutsu. No, these are not dishes sort of the golden dragon Chinese restaurant. No offense, I miss you, shall I? None taken, sir. I'm Japanese.

Yeah, well, whatever. Anyway, these are combat techniques that can save your life. As they did for me, when I was in Forest Range Ring, Quebec. Really? What happened? That night, I was quite enjoying the waffle with maple syrup by my campfire. When I was attacked by a vicious beaver, it's razor sharp teeth. We're going for my jugger, but I was able to save myself thanks to my perfect mastery of the raging enemy full-time.

That's so funny about that. It takes Jesus. Seriously, I think you would have been better off not talking about it. Right. Okay, then. Enough laughing. Let's get into groups of two. We're good for a beginner.
Not bad, but you can both use a lot more work. I'll do it for tonight, kids. How do we each other now and go get some beauty sleep? Well, nice to meet you, too. Huh?

What is with this mess? Where did that mud come from? Uh, that's Kiwi, my dog. He just had a little fit. That's all. You understand? The four things not used to being cooped up. I mean look at this. My scene is my clothes. He stretched everything in any dog. Did you see what he did in my bed? Ah, I'm sorry, but I couldn't take him out with everyone around. There are no dogs, a lot of cats. No kidding. I think the principle is going to be interesting hearing about this. That's it, Kiwi. I'm a little diggy-dug.
What's going on? I've seen that logo before.

Your little friends are kind of aggressive. Maybe you should check their settings. I had nothing to do with it. They just attacked me all of a sudden. He lost me. She might be explaining. No, forget it. Come on, tell me. Maybe I can be of some help. What if you suddenly get attacked by a hairdryer? I'm not around. Ha, very funny. I'm not leaving this room until I know what's up. Okay. What about got to lose? I'm already in over my heads. I might as well tell you. This is so crazy.
First, tell me if you can keep a secret. That tunnel in the gym is a great way to beat curfew. Yeah. And there's another one on the campus as well. Yeah. What's the matter? Scared of heights? No.

Okay, so that's the mega computer you started up? Uh-huh. It's actually a super computer. It's ultra-powerful. And while I was examining it, I found out that it runs a virtual universe called Yoko. You know, what Velpua? This morning's electric shock fried your brain. This seems just the control center of the factory, that's all. Oh, yeah? And what would you call this, huh? A program for spray painting doors? Hello, Jeremy.

Who's the girl with the pink hair? That's Maya, a form of artificial intelligence who lives on Lyoko. I don't know what she's there for. Yeah. Don't mess with me. You programmed her, right? No. I woke up the moment that Jeremy restarted the super computer. I don't know who I am. My memory has been erased. Maya has to hide in some kind of a tower. As soon as she leaves it, she gets attacked by monsters. You've gotta believe me, Stern. This is an incredible discovery. Let's say this.

What if it's just some kind of giant video game? Then it's a very dangerous game. My robots are programmed just to go and fetch a ball, and they just try to kill me don't forget. You saw them! What's more, the moment they attack, a kind of logo appeared on my computer screen, the same one that the monsters had on Lyoko.

bit her and better. Do you mean then that the virtual thing that attacked Maya also wants to kill you? That's what it looks like. You know what? If that's true about what? If it's that dangerous, I think you should shut it down and call the police. Okay. But first, I want to help Maya to understand what she's doing on Lyoko. See what? I really do. And if I'm right, we can materialize her right here on Earth. Oh, that's too much. What makes you think you can? You'll see, Star.

This is what is absolutely perfect! Tonight, my dear old Rick, you're all mine! What's all this? Scanners! I still haven't found the program that can bring Maya back to Earth, but I dug up the one which allows someone to be scanned and sent to Lyoko. In fact, the supercomputer analyzes your molecular structure through these cabots and then breaks down your atoms before digitalizing them and recreating a digital incarnation in the virtual world.

In English, you go inside the cabin, your teleported to the virtual world. Jeremy these things don't exist yet. There's no way. You still don't want to believe me. I would love to, but virtualization is straight out of science fiction. Why don't we try it out? That way, you can see for yourself. We're going to need a kidney pig. A kidney pig? No problem.
Well, got us a prize for you, don't you? You're going to be virtualized. Hey! Hey! Hey!
Hey! Oh, it's coming out! What is it going? I'm sure there's another girl behind all this. No, look! It's not a girl! It's Kiwi! My dog!

But why is it taking killings at this factory? Oh, no! Maybe he wants to use him for some horrible science experiment. You watch too many horror movies. Yeah, you're right. That was pretty dumb. It's probably more like some sort of ritual sacrifice or something. Nice, but no move. Everything's cool, Jeremy. The mutt is in the scanner. Okay, let's see how it goes. It's not going to be a piece of cake.
Those things look like offense. You're right! Maybe it's a tomb of an Egyptian funeral, huh? Right. You were all set. I'm starting up the procedure. Can you read? I don't care, boy.

Oh no! You're going to change my profile! Transfer Guinea Pig! Scanner! Guinea Pig! Wait! That's a weird looking dog! That's no dog! That's not dog! Oh yeah! I don't know how to stop the procedure! Man! Ouch! What's going on around here? Where am I?

And why am I dressed up like a giant purple cat? Odello, Robya? Uh-oh. Who's calling me? It's me, Jeremy Belpoz. Is this a joke or something? Uh, no. Yeah. Cissy? Oh! Oh, it's boring. It's okay. Od is safe and sound on LeoCo.
Am I in a video game? It looks like the set for Babylon 1 Ninja Fighters 3. Actually, I don't really know. It's a virtual world that much I'm sure of. Cool!

Oh, that's a lot less cool. And there are these giant cockroaches attacking me. Is that going on? Yeah. Oh, nothing to worry about them. That's not what I meant. You got to get out of there. Poor it. Delorobia's in danger. And I need time to de-virtualize him. I don't know what to do. He needs help. Do you think you can send CC and me to Leo Coel?

I can always try. What? Isn't this weird? Oh, right? To a virtual world where weird monsters want to massacre you. You wanted to go out with me and now you're a chance. No, thanks. I prefer real worlds. I'm not into the other kind of class. I couldn't possibly mischillating practice tomorrow morning. See you. Chicken. Jeremy, I'm ready to go. OK. I'm starting the procedure. Transfer, orange. Get her orange.
Virtualization! Ah! Hang in there! Well, we're sure it's on its way to give you a hand! Great! I was starting to get bored here all alone! Wow! This sword rocks!

Hey, that's not fair. How come he gets to be a samurai? That's so much cooler. I have no idea why. Maybe the computer just reads into your own subconscious desires and projects them onto your digital incarnation. I don't dream about giant purple cats. Once more, I don't know they'll have a weapon like Oric. Look, these great big useless paws. Hey, what's wrong?

These arrows are pretty cool. I take back what I said. Wow! Try to hook up with Maya in her tower. Hold on, I'll give you the coordinates. Hold this Maya! And what is that thing? It's the main frame of a super computer.

Hey in there, Jeremy! Yeah! Yeah! Yeah! Yeah! And who's the second time? Are you talking about the end of the day you wouldn't be able to do without me?

And they may be the way to materialize you on Earth. Unbelievable, huh? I can't wait to tell the others about it.

You want us to go back to Lyoko to get Maya into a red tower? Like I said, I'm sure that's the only way to bring her into the real world. Pretty wild, huh? Pretty dangerous, you mean? We could have all been wiped out for good last night. That's right. We're better off shutting down the super computer. Too bad for Maya, but still it's not like she's human. But she's still a sort of intelligent being. She's got emotions like we do. You see, I told her, he's madly in love with a computer program. Come on, guys. Be cool. I'm just asking you to go there one last time.

We can't even make it past the monsters. We'll probably get ourselves blown away in a heartbeat. Really, there were really three of us, but Cissy's too scared. But, no, I'm not. I really do want to go with you guys till the alcohol, but when cheerleading practices over. Thanks, Cissy. So here's the scoop. As soon as Maya's materialized into the real world, I give you my word that I'll shut down the supercomputer. Meanwhile, we gotta keep this a secret. Promise. I swear. Get out.

I can't wait to see what I look like on the Oco. Me too. With some luck, I won't be the dumbest looking one in the group. Oh Rick, where are you going? Aren't you coming to see my charity routine? No, I got something important to take care of. Back for another thrashing. I don't like ending on a bad note. That's good. I like a challenge. You're gonna get one.
Yeah! Yeah!
One, two, three, four, one, two, three, four. This is gonna blow their mind. Jeremy, Jeremy can hear me. Jeremy?

Meanwhile, if you don't stay away from me, my father's gonna find out that you two clowns tried to break into my room.
She's even more beautiful when she's angry, isn't she?
A couple of days ago, I deck up a brand new program on the supercomputer called Return to the Past, and I just figured out how to activate it.

Then, you say that electrochemistry involves opposing polar systems composed at its two extremes of electrically conductive material.

Yeah! Call it a tie? Okay. Hey, I gotta go. My friends are gonna start wondering where I am. Same for you. I don't have any friends. Whoa! What is that thing? No time to explain. Come on, we gotta get out of here.

You're the one who brought Elizabeth here. What happened? She was in the room, and she was attacked by this electricity monster that jumped from a co-dinger. Would you try to be serious polyacoph? You're the one who's going to get a shock in a moment.

We have to get to Lyoko and bring her to the red tower. If it works, we can shut the computer down tonight. We'll meet you in the sewer.

Sorry, Yumi. But I've got to go. Hang on. You seem to know what's going on here. What was this electrical thing that attacked us? And what is this supercomputer you're talking about? Look, I don't have time to explain. OK. You have time for a third round then? Here's stubborn as a meal, aren't you? That's right. And since I have no friends, I've lots of time. Come on, I'll explain them away.
Hey! Who's that girl? This is Yumi. She was there when I was attacked and she knows how to fight so it's okay. Okay, okay later. Right now, we have to get to the factory. Let's go!
Don't tell me you're scared.

So who's this Maya? You're going to be meeting her in just a minute. Go straight to the skater room and get ready for the big plunge. Big plunge, but I don't have a bathing suit. Relax! You won't be needing one.

No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No! No!

Yeah!

If you really think you can do it, they don't call me Einstein for nothing. In a few weeks, I'll have to figure it out. Meanwhile, let's not forget about our pat, huh? Hey, relax. We all know how to keep secret.
